release-date.comment: Public availability May 2024 per CNX-Software launch coverage.

status.comment: Listed as current product on official BPI shop and forum (checked 2026-04-26).

open-source-hardware.comment: Marketed as open-source hardware, but vendor publishes only schematics PDFs and references. Per Assessment Guidelines, schematics-only does not qualify; could not confirm full PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license. Marking null pending verification.

country-of-origin.comment: SINOVOIP / Banana Pi is headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

cpu-cores.comment: Two clusters of 4× SpacemiT X60; only Cluster0 carries the AI extensions.

cpu-architecture.comment: Asymmetric pair of clusters (4+4) of identical X60 cores; Cluster0 has the 2 TOPS AI extension, Cluster1 does not.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: K1 silicon is rated up to 2.0 GHz, but BPI-F3 ships clocked at 1.6 GHz per CNX-Software and community measurements.

process-node.comment: Not officially disclosed by SpacemiT for the K1; community references vary. Using null per Assessment Guidelines for unverifiable Chinese-SoC nodes.

vulkan-support.comment: IMG BXE-2-32 supports Vulkan 1.2 (some sources cite 1.3); driver maturity on RISC-V Linux varies.

npu.comment: AI extension is part of Cluster0 of the X60 cores rather than a discrete NPU block, but vendor markets it as NPU-class.

npu-performance-tops.comment: Vendor-quoted 2.0 TOPS at INT8 (per usual SBC convention).

hw-video-decode.comment: VPU supports H.264/H.265/VP9/VP8 4K dec; AV1 is not advertised. VP8 has no defined tag, omitted.

hw-video-encode.comment: Encode covers H.264/H.265/VP9/VP8 per CNX-Software launch article. VP8 has no defined tag, omitted.

ram-type.comment: LPDDR4/4X soldered; vendor labels as LPDDR4.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 Key M slot with PCIe lanes; supports NVMe SSDs.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; SATA only via M.2 + JMB582-style adapter.

bluetooth.comment: Vendor wiki specifies BT 4.2 even though similar AP6256 SiP modules can do BT 5.x; trusting vendor.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: 1× USB 2.0 Type-C OTG (not high-speed); no USB-C power delivery role.

poe-support.comment: PoE only via add-on BPI-5400B PoE HAT/module; not built-in to the SBC itself.

max-display-resolution.comment: HDMI output capped at 1080p60 even though VPU decodes 4K.

displayport.comment: No native DP and Type-C is USB 2.0 OTG only (no DP-Alt mode).

audio-jack.comment: Speaker, microphone, and earphone (3.5 mm) jacks present.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: 26-pin header, not the modern Raspberry Pi 40-pin layout.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: 26-pin header and 148×100 mm board do not match Pi mechanical or HAT EEPROM standards.

pcie-lanes-exposed.value: M.2 Key M (PCIe 2.1) + Mini-PCIe — up to 5 PCIe 2.1 lanes total exposed (2+2+1)
pcie-lanes-exposed.comment: Vendor advertises a 5-lane PCIe 2.1 fanout split as 2+2+1 across M.2 Key M, Mini-PCIe, and an additional connector.

idle-power-w.comment: Community measurement on 8 GB variant under Bianbu 3.0.1: 2.64–4.78 W idle without peripherals (using midpoint ~3.5 W); checked 2026-04-26.

load-power-w.comment: LinuxLinks measurement under s-tui CPU stress, 7.1 W (compared with OrangePi RV2 at 6.4 W).

cooling-required.comment: Vendor ships boards with no required cooling but recommends a heatsink for sustained loads; ~7 W max draw is well within passive territory but K1 thermals warrant a heatsink.

rtc-battery-header.comment: Onboard RTC interface for connecting a coin-cell battery.

form-factor-class.comment: 148×100 mm is close to Pico-ITX (100×72) only along one axis; closer to a custom router-style PCB. Tagged pico-itx as the closest defined class but it is not a strict match.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: 148×100 mm board does not match Pi-standard mounting holes.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: SpacemiT K1 support landed in mainline (Linux 6.14+ adds CPU/early enablement); board boots upstream but GPU, NPU, and several K1 peripherals still rely on vendor 6.1.15 BSP for full functionality.

official-os-image.comment: Vendor-maintained Bianbu Linux images (and Debian/Armbian builds) published on the wiki.

distros-with-images.comment: Bianbu (vendor Debian-derivative), Armbian, Ubuntu 24.04, and Fedora RISC-V images all publish board-specific BPI-F3 builds.

bootloader.comment: U-Boot + OpenSBI per vendor source tree.

long-term-support.comment: Vendor has not stated a formal LTS window; SINOVOIP historically rolls support into community/Armbian. Industrial-grade marketing implies multi-year availability but no explicit commitment.

android-support.comment: No official or community Android image released as of 2026-04-26.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Official BPI shop price for 2 GB LPDDR4 + 8 GB eMMC base config; checked 2026-04-26.

msrp-top-usd.comment: Top 16 GB / 128 GB eMMC SKU price not currently advertised on the official BPI shop product page; resellers list ~$130–160 but vendor price not confirmed.

available-regions.comment: Sold globally via BPI shop, AliExpress, Amazon US, and major resellers.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at BPI shop and Amazon; checked 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Vendor wiki and docs site cover specs and getting-started, schematics PDF available, but coverage is uneven and some pages are stubs. Adequate but not exhaustive.

community-size.comment: Active BPI forum sub-category and Armbian thread; sits between niche RISC-V SBCs and large RPi-ecosystem boards.

active-forum-discord.comment: Vendor-run forum has an active BPI-F3 sub-section.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Limited official accessories (PoE module, cases). 26-pin non-Pi header rules out the broad RPi HAT ecosystem.
